title: Garlic Chilli Chicken Wrap
tagline: Pan-charred garlic chilli chicken rolled into an egg wrap — a street-style kathi roll.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients, assuming shop-bought pre-marinated garlic chilli chicken.
The mayo and the egg carry most of the fat. Swap mayo for hung curd or Greek-yogurt mint sauce to drop ~80 kcal and ~9 g fat per wrap while keeping it creamy.
Already solid protein (~39 g/wrap). For more, use egg whites only (skip yolks) and pack in 40-50 g extra chicken, or add a slice of paneer.

items: 350 g pre-marinated garlic chilli chicken (shop-bought, boneless)
3-4 tbsp water (to rescue the marinade from burning)
1 tsp butter + 1 clove garlic, optional finish
Sliced onion + a squeeze of lemon, optional

steps.title: Sear the chicken
steps.body: Put the 350 g pre-marinated chicken into a hot pan in a single layer and sear ~3 minutes on the first side without moving it. Flip and sear the other side 3 minutes. You want browning and some char — that's flavour, not a mistake.

steps.title: The key fix — drop the heat and add water
steps.body: The marinade (sugar and spices) burns fast on high heat. As soon as both sides are seared, lower the heat to LOW, add 3-4 tbsp water and cover with a lid. This stops the burning, loosens the caramelised marinade, and cooks the inside through.

steps.title: Cook it through
steps.body: Let it cook covered on low for 5-6 minutes. Cut open the biggest piece — if it's white with clear juices and no pink, it's done. If slightly pink, add 2 more tbsp water and cook 2 more minutes.

steps.title: Optional finish
steps.body: For proper garlic chilli chicken, toss in 1 tsp butter + 1 minced garlic clove at the end and let it coat the pieces for 30 seconds.

steps.title: Make the egg wrap
steps.body: Wipe the pan, add 1/2 tsp oil on medium heat and pour in 1 beaten egg. Immediately lay a roti on top of the wet egg and press so it sticks. Flip after ~30 seconds so the roti side warms. Repeat for the second wrap.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Egg-side up, spread 1 tbsp mayo (or mint hung-curd sauce), add half the chicken, jalapeños, and onion + lemon if using. Roll tight.

steps.title: Toast the roll
steps.body: Give the rolled wrap a final toast in the dry pan, seam-side down, for a crisp shell that locks everything in. Slice and eat hot.

notes: The whole technique here is sear -> low cook -> repurpose: start on lower heat, add the 2-3 tbsp water EARLY to save the marinade, and finish with butter + garlic for restaurant-level flavour.
A well-charred marinade tastes smoky and tandoori-like — that char is the good kind, not burnt.
Capsicum + onion sautéed alongside, or a squeeze of lemon at the end, both level it up.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
